+### Experiment 5b — CSS-shape markers, reusing 2b's background-layer mechanism
+
+**Verdict: keep**, pending the head-to-head against 5a. Branched off
+`experiment/decorations-2b-guides-css-gradient`. All 9 corpus fixtures (the shared 8, plus
+a `quote`-kind fixture promoted into `ALL_DECORATION_FIXTURES` per this experiment's own
+shared prerequisite) screenshotted, both bundled themes, plus targeted computed-style
+assertions on the resolved `::after` background-image — see
+[e2e/specs/52-block-markers-shapes.e2e.ts](../../e2e/specs/52-block-markers-shapes.e2e.ts),
+71/71 e2e tests green across the full suite (all 8 spec files, including a dedicated
+"marker vertical position" sub-suite added after real-vault review — see bug 5 below,
+two pixel-exact guide/marker-alignment tests, and a fold-chevron-clearance sub-suite —
+see the follow-ups below), plus 127/127 unit tests (`npm test`), including new
+`decorate: kind`/`headingGuideDepths` tests. Confirmed live
+on 4 real vault notes (headings, paragraphs, lists, checkboxes, wikilinks, a code block, a
+callout, a table) — no defects found; markers coexist cleanly with real content, including
+multi-line paragraphs and wikilinks immediately after a marked line.
+
+**Design pivoted once, after a live look at the first version.** The plan's own design
+called for a distinct shape+color per eligible kind (8 marks: dot/ring/square/diamond/
+plus/tick/wedge/cross across Obsidian's 8 accent colors) — built, screenshotted, and
+initially judged reasonable from a distance. On closer real-content review the user's
+verdict was that the variety read as **cryptic, not helpful**: eight different shapes and
+colors ask a reader to memorize a legend before the marks mean anything, which is the
+opposite of legible. Replaced with a single, uniform, solid dot — same color as the guide
+lines themselves (`var(--text-faint)`) — for every eligible kind. This is a real design
+lesson worth carrying forward past this one experiment: for a piece of chrome whose whole
+job is "signal that a node starts here," uniformity communicates that job better than
+variety that has no established meaning yet. The per-kind shape system is described below
+only for the record (what was tried and why it was reverted), not as the current design —
+current code has exactly one shape.
+
+**Mechanism (current)**: a single `radial-gradient` dot (`markerBackground()` in
+`decorations.ts`), same color as guides, one call site for every eligible kind (heading/
+paragraph/code/table/callout/quote/html/hr — everything except list-item, which needs no
+marker check at all now: eligibility collapsed to "not a list item," full stop, since
+every other `NodeKind` gets the identical mark). Exposed as a new `--to-marker` custom
+property, painted through the *exact* same `::after` 2b's guides already use
+(`background: var(--to-marker, none), var(--to-guides, none);`) — zero new
+pseudo-element, zero new DOM, zero new `ViewPlugin`. Gap lines stay explicitly
+marker-free (they carry no `decorate()` fact/kind at all — verified by a dedicated e2e
+test).
+
+**The real integration wrinkle, exactly as flagged going in**: `lineDecoration()`'s
+`to-decor-guides` gate (driving `position: relative` and the whole `::after` rule) was
+guide-only (`guideDepths.length > 0`); changed to `guideDepths.length > 0 || hasMarker` so
+a depth-0 node with zero ancestors (e.g. a bare top-level heading, which has no guide of
+its own today) still shows a marker — confirmed live and by a dedicated e2e test
+(`getLinePseudoComputedStyle` reads a non-empty resolved background at depth 0). This
+touches the shared `MarginCompensation` `ViewPlugin` too (for widget-rendered atoms:
+table/callout/html/hr), which needed the identical gate change plus setting `--to-marker`
+alongside its existing `--to-guides` handling.
+
+**Bugs found and fixed** (all caught only by actually looking at a rendered screenshot,
+not by any DOM/computed-style assertion — directly reinforcing this project's recurring
+lesson that visual claims need an actual look, not box-model reasoning alone):
+
+1. **Depth-0 markers were completely invisible, caught by zooming into a screenshot of
+   the `mixed` fixture's top-level `"# Top"` heading.** The marker's `background-position`
+   is deliberately negative at shallow depths (it sits `MARKER_GAP` left of the node's own
+   indent column, which is column 0 at depth 0) — but a background can *never* paint
+   outside its own element's box, regardless of what any ancestor's `overflow`/`contain`
+   allows. This is a **stricter** rule than the one guides rely on: guides' own doc comment
+   established that a pseudo's box can be *widened* via `left`/`right` to reach a shallower
+   column, but a first pass here wrongly assumed the same finding covered a marker's own
+   small negative position too, without the box actually being widened for it. Fixed by
+   computing `markerShortfall(depth)` — `max(0px, MARKER_RESERVE − depth × unit)`, the same
+   `max(0px, ...)` clamp idiom Experiment 1's own table-padding fix established — and
+   folding it into `--to-own-shift` (which now activates whenever `ownShiftUnits > 0 ||
+   marker`, not just for margin-shifted kinds) *and* into every guide layer's own position
+   formula active on the same line (`guideLayer` gained an `extra` parameter), since the
+   pseudo's local coordinate origin is one shared thing for the whole box — widening it for
+   the marker's sake shifts everything else painted in that same box too. At this module's
+   default sizes, only depth 0 actually needs the extra reach — confirmed live that every
+   depth ≥ 1 marker rendered correctly even before this fix, which is exactly why the bug
+   surfaced only on the flat/mixed fixtures' top-level nodes and not on any nested one.
+2. **(Per-kind design, since reverted) The table's diamond mark rendered as a lopsided
+   pinwheel**, caught by zooming into a screenshot of the `widget-atoms` fixture — the
+   standard 4-gradient diamond recipe requires every quadrant's gradient to list
+   `transparent 50%` before the color, consistently across all four; an earlier version
+   flipped that order on two of the four quadrants, producing a shape with the wrong
+   halves filled.
+3. **(Per-kind design, since reverted) The callout's plus-sign rendered as a lopsided "⊥"**
+   (vertical bar with the crossbar near its bottom, not its middle), caught by zooming into
+   the same `widget-atoms` screenshot. Root cause: the vertical-centering formula used `%`,
+   and `background-position`'s `%` component resolves relative to *that specific layer's
+   own image size* subtracted from the box — which differed between the vertical bar (a
+   full-height image) and the horizontal bar (a much thinner one), so the "same" `50%`
+   position landed at two different absolute heights for the two layers making up one
+   shape. Invisible on single-line plain `.cm-line`s (box height ≈ marker size, so the
+   discrepancy was too small to notice) but glaring on a widget atom whose box spans its
+   *entire* rendered height (a multi-line callout, not just its title line).
+4. **Heading markers sat noticeably above the native fold chevron on H1–H5, and merely
+   collided with it on H6 — reported directly by the user after a real-vault look, not
+   caught by any fixture or assertion.** The fix for bug 3 above replaced `%`-based
+   vertical centering with a FIXED length from the box's own top, chosen to solve the
+   widget-atom case — but a fixed length doesn't scale with a *heading's own* line-height,
+   which varies by level (H1's line box is taller than H6's). Measured live, not guessed:
+   `.cm-fold-indicator`'s own `getBoundingClientRect()` against its line's, for H1 through
+   H6 in a real running Obsidian instance. Two findings: the chevron's own vertical
+   *center* sits at a level-dependent ~67–70% down the line box (not a clean, reusable
+   constant), but its **top** is a robust, level-independent constant — exactly 1rem below
+   the line's own top at every level measured — because the chevron's position is fixed
+   while only *its own icon size* (not its position) scales with the heading's font-size.
+   First fix: gave heading lines specifically a measured fixed-top anchor (`1rem`, matching
+   the chevron's own measured top). Confirmed live via screenshot — no longer "above" on
+   any level — but this fix didn't survive the NEXT round of real-vault review (bug 5).
+5. **The whole "one CSS formula per box-type" approach was still wrong — a full-suite
+   real-vault review found FIVE distinct placement failures at once, all reported directly
+   by the user, none caught by any fixture/assertion.** Headings still sat above their own
+   text (the bug-4 fixed-top anchor didn't move with a font-size-driven line-height
+   *increase* the same way the chevron's OWN top didn't, but the user's own read of "middle
+   of the heading text" wanted the TEXT's center, and a bare fixed length can't track a
+   font-size it has no knowledge of at layout time); single-line paragraphs sat at their own
+   top edge (the still-present double-`HALF`-subtraction bug from bug 3, never actually
+   fixed for the plain-line case, only worked around for widget atoms); wrapped multi-row
+   paragraphs/blockquotes centered on their ENTIRE wrapped height instead of just the first
+   visual row; callouts and code blocks sat at the literal top of the whole block instead of
+   near their own first content row. Root cause, named directly by the user: **no CSS-only
+   formula (percentage, fixed length, or any mix) can know where "the first rendered visual
+   row of this specific box's content" actually is** — that's fundamentally a *rendering*
+   fact, not a static layout constant, and every earlier fix in this area was really just a
+   differently-shaped guess at it. Fixed by abandoning CSS-only positioning for `Y` entirely
+   and measuring it live: `MarginCompensation` (already the established "StateField draws a
+   default, ViewPlugin patches in the true live-read value" mechanism for `nativeMarginBasePx`
+   and table-padding compensation) now computes a real per-line pixel offset via
+   `document.createRange().selectNodeContents(lineEl); range.getClientRects()[0]` — the DOM's
+   own notion of an element's first wrapped visual row, which handles word-wrap for free and
+   scales with whatever font-size is actually in effect, no guessing required. Written to a
+   new `--to-decor-marker-y` custom property; `markerBackground()` itself only ever emits a
+   `var(--to-decor-marker-y, 50%)` reference (the `50%` fallback is now CORRECTLY centering,
+   not double-subtracted, for the brief window before the ViewPlugin's own pass overrides
+   it). Widget atoms get the same technique aimed at a kind-appropriate reference element
+   instead of the widget's own root (a callout's `.callout-title`, a table's first `<tr>`;
+   `hr` has no text content at all, so its own half-height stands in; `html`, arbitrary raw
+   markup with no fixed structure, falls back to the same first-row Range technique applied
+   to its own root). One deliberate, documented exception: a code fence's structural first
+   `.cm-line` is the OPENING marker row (confirmed live to be a REAL, normal-height row —
+   not collapsed to zero — that Live Preview just renders with nothing but a language badge
+   on it, with the actual code text starting on the NEXT line down) — reaching into that next
+   line for a "true" measurement was rejected because this pseudo-element's own box is
+   exactly ONE line tall and a background can never paint outside its own box (the same hard
+   rule bug 1 already established), so doing this safely would need the same kind of
+   vertical box-widening `--to-own-shift` already does horizontally, introducing an unverified
+   NEW paint-order risk against the next (opaque-backgrounded) code line. Per an explicit user
+   ask ("if that is harder to calculate, let's try an offset, to make it a bit lower"), code
+   fences instead bias toward the bottom of their own (still fully in-bounds, zero clipping
+   risk) opener row via a plain `95%` — confirmed live via computed style (`background-position:
+   12px 95%`) to land within ~1px of the next line's own top, a pragmatic near-miss rather than
+   an exact, mechanism-stretching alignment. All five cases now covered by dedicated e2e tests
+   (see `52-block-markers-shapes.e2e.ts`'s "marker vertical position" suite) reading the
+   resolved `background-position` directly, not just the raw custom property.
+
+**Code cost (current, single-dot design + live-measured Y)**: ~110 lines added to
+`decorations.ts` (marker position/color constants, `markerBackground()`, `firstRowCenterPx`/
+`widgetMarkerYPx`, and the `lineDecoration()`/`MarginCompensation` integration — still
+meaningfully SMALLER than the original 8-shape version's ~150 lines despite the added
+live-measurement code, since one shape needs no per-kind switch, no quadrant/tick/bar helper
+functions, and no color table), ~1 line changed in `styles.css` (the `::after` rule's
+`background` shorthand, plus an expanded doc comment), ~10 lines added to `decorate.ts`
+(`kind` field on `LineDecorationFact` — still used by `MarginCompensation`'s widget-Y
+dispatch and the code-fence Y exception, even though `markerBackground()` itself no longer
+branches on kind). Reuses 100% of 2b's existing plumbing (custom property → single
+`::after`, `--to-own-shift` compensation, `MarginCompensation`'s widget-DOM patching loop)
+for X-axis positioning and shape/color — no new CM6 mechanism, no new pseudo-element, no
+new `ViewPlugin`. Y-axis positioning is a GENUINELY new piece of live-measurement code
+(not reused from guides, which never needed it), the first real net-new mechanism this
+experiment added rather than reused. The one real *design* cost independent of Y (not
+code-size cost) is that `--to-own-shift` and every guide layer's X position formula are no
+longer independent of whether a marker is *also* active on the same line — a coupling 5a's
+separate-DOM-element mechanism wouldn't have, since a real DOM child element doesn't need
+to share a single pseudo-element's one coordinate space with the guide gradient.
+
+**Known limitation, not fixed on this branch**: markers and guides share one `::after`'s
+single `opacity: 0.6`, inherited from the guide rule (subtle-on-purpose, for a background
+grid). A marker dot at 60% opacity is legible but visibly softer than surrounding text —
+CSS has no way to give two background layers on the *same* element independent opacity.
+Splitting them (e.g. painting the marker at full opacity some other way) would cost exactly
+the "claim a third pseudo-element" move this experiment's own design explicitly ruled out
+as unavailable. Worth a deliberate follow-up decision (accept the shared opacity, or
+promote markers off `::after` onto a real DOM node as 5a already does) once both variants
+are compared side by side.
+
+**Follow-up refinement: guide columns now align with a marker's own CENTER, not the raw
+depth boundary**, requested by the user to read more like native nested lists (a connecting
+line running straight through each bullet, not stopping short of it). Previously a guide at
+ancestor depth `d` was drawn at column `d * unit` exactly — the same column the NEXT depth
+level's indentation starts at, which is also where a marker's own reach calculation
+happened to START (its left edge), not where it visually CENTERS. This left a real, visible
+gap: the guide line sat to the right of the marker dot above/below it, a seam rather than a
+continuous line. Fixed by shifting every guide's own column left by `MARKER_HALF +
+MARKER_GAP` (`GUIDE_COLUMN_OFFSET`) — exactly the offset between a marker's own depth
+column and its rendered center (confirmed live, pixel-exact: a dedicated e2e test reads
+both a heading's own marker center and its descendant's guide column as independent
+absolute screen positions — accounting for the pseudo-element's own `left` CSS offset
+*and* `background-position`, a real bug in the test's first draft, not the implementation —
+and asserts they match within 1px).
+
+This shift itself went negative for shallow (in practice, depth-0) ancestors, the same
+"background can't paint outside its own box" constraint markers already ran into — fixed by
+generalizing the existing `markerShortfall`/`--to-own-shift` widening mechanism
+(`combineExtra`) to also cover the shallowest active guide's own reach, not just a line's
+own marker. The two are independent reasons a line's pseudo box might need widening (its
+OWN marker's left edge; a shallow ancestor's now marker-center-aligned guide column) that
+happen to share one box, so `combineExtra` takes the `max()` of whichever apply. This also
+meant a blank trailingGap line (which never carries a marker, only ever a guide) can now
+independently need `--to-own-shift` too — previously true only for a small minority of
+gap lines with genuinely shallow guides, but the mechanism generalizes without needing a
+gap-specific carve-out.
+
+**Real bug shipped in the above fix, caught by the user from a real-vault screenshot
+(not by the dedicated alignment test) — mid-chain markers drifted visibly left of their
+own guide column.** The screenshot showed a real note (`Wednesday — review day` → `Aurora
+review` → paragraphs): the FIRST guide (from the top-level heading) correctly passed
+through the second level's marker, but every level from there down drifted — "the bullets
+(from the second level on) are shifted to the left, while the guides are evenly spaced,"
+in the user's own words, correctly diagnosing that the guides themselves were fine and the
+markers were the ones off. Root cause: the fix above computes ONE combined `extra` per
+line — `max` of the line's own marker shortfall and its shallowest active guide's shortfall
+— and that combined value is what the box is ACTUALLY widened by (`--to-own-shift`). The
+marker's own X-position formula must use that SAME combined value for the cancellation
+algebra to hold (`--to-own-shift` and the position formula need to widen/shift by identical
+amounts, or the difference leaks into the rendered position) — but the code built the
+marker's background string from its own (narrower) `markerShortfall` alone, computed
+*before* combining with the guide's requirement, then combined separately only for
+`--to-own-shift`. For a depth-0 node this is invisible (no guide of its own exists to
+diverge from, so the two values coincide) — exactly the case the dedicated alignment test
+used, which is why it passed despite the bug. For a MID-chain node like "Aurora review"
+(depth 1, carries both its own marker AND an active depth-0 guide bridging through it),
+its own shortfall alone is 0 (depth 1 doesn't need widening for its OWN marker), but the
+combined value is nonzero (the depth-0 guide does need it) — using the narrower value for
+the marker's position while the box widens by the larger one drifted the marker left by
+exactly `MARKER_RESERVE` (~12px at default sizes), confirmed by hand-deriving the algebra
+and matching it against live computed-style output before fixing. Fixed by computing the
+combined `extra` FIRST and using it for both the box widening AND the marker's own X
+formula. New e2e test added specifically for this shape (heading → heading → paragraph,
+three levels) rather than trusting the depth-0/depth-1 case again — verifies every
+ancestor's marker center against its immediate child's guide column, not just the
+top-level pair. A concrete, second instance of this project's own recurring lesson: a
+real multi-level scenario is a different (and necessary) test from its simplest two-level
+stand-in, and a screenshot a human actually looked at caught what a passing assertion
+suite didn't.
+
+**Follow-up: the native fold chevron overlapped our marker on headings, fixed with a
+second, real multi-part bug of its own.** Reported directly by the user: Obsidian's own
+fold/collapse indicator (`.collapse-indicator.collapse-icon` — the actual glyph; its
+wrapper, `.cm-fold-indicator`, measured 0 width live and isn't itself a useful reference)
+sits in the exact same pixels our marker does, on every heading (confirmed live: the
+chevron's own gutter is reserved even on a heading with no children yet, painted at
+`opacity: 0` until hovered/foldable — so the reach is needed unconditionally, not only once
+a heading actually has a child, which also avoids the marker jumping sideways the moment
+one is added). Its own width doesn't vary by heading level (confirmed live, H1 through H3
+identical in the bundled theme), unlike its vertical position, so it's measured ONCE per
+render from any one heading line and applied uniformly — the same "measure a single
+representative reference" pattern `nativeMarginBasePx` already established, not a new one.
+
+Fixed by folding this reach into the SAME `markerShortfall`/`--to-own-shift` mechanism
+markers already use (not a parallel, independently-applied offset — an earlier draft of
+this exact fix tried that shape and it silently double-subtracted). Two real bugs shipped
+in getting there, both caught by the user pushing on the result rather than by any
+assertion in this experiment's own suite at the time:
+
+1. **Shifting a heading's own marker left of the chevron broke every DESCENDANT's guide
+   column, which still pointed at the marker's OLD (un-shifted) center.** A first version
+   only recomputed the fold-adjusted marker on the heading's OWN line, live, via
+   `MarginCompensation` — correct for that one line in isolation, but guides (already fixed
+   to align with a marker's CENTER, see above) are computed independently on every
+   DESCENDANT line, with no way to know the ancestor's marker had moved. Root cause,
+   structurally: only HEADINGS (and list items, which never own a guide at all) can fold in
+   Obsidian's own UI — a `paragraph`-owned guide (this project's tree lets a paragraph have
+   children too) never needs the extra reach, so the fix couldn't be a single per-line
+   constant; it needed to know, PER ACTIVE GUIDE DEPTH on a given line, whether THAT
+   specific depth's owning ancestor is a heading. Fixed by extending `computeLineGuides`
+   (decorate.ts) with a new `headingGuideDepths` field — the subset of `guideDepths` whose
+   owner is a heading — and generalizing `guideShortfall`/`guideLayer`/`guideBackground`/
+   `combineExtra` to accept a per-depth fold-gap reach instead of one shared value. Checking
+   only the shallowest active guide depth (the pre-existing optimization, valid without
+   fold-gap since shortfall strictly decreases with depth) also had to go: fold-gap breaks
+   that monotonicity, since it applies only to SOME depths — a deeper heading-owned guide
+   can now need more reach than a shallower paragraph-owned one on the same line, so
+   `combineExtra` checks every active depth, not just the first. The live-measurement pass
+   in `MarginCompensation` was correspondingly widened from "the heading's own line only" to
+   "every line that carries a marker OR bridges a heading-owned guide" — consolidated
+   through one new shared function, `computeMarkerAndGuideBg`, so `lineDecoration()`,
+   `gapLineDecoration()`, the widget loop, and this live pass all funnel through the exact
+   same computation (foldGapPx defaulting to 0 for the three static callers) rather than
+   four independently-maintained copies.
+2. **The fix for bug 1 then dropped the `ownShiftUnits * unit` term for margin-shifted
+   lines (atoms/list items) when recomputing `--to-own-shift` live**, caught by a dedicated
+   e2e test (not a screenshot this time — the discrepancy was small enough, and the
+   fixture specific enough, that a computed-style assertion was the right tool) failing by
+   exactly one `--to-decor-unit` (24px at default sizes): `--to-own-shift` for these kinds
+   must equal `ownShiftUnits * unit + extra` (their OWN depth-based margin shift, plus the
+   marker/guide widening) — the new general live-override path set it to `extra` alone,
+   which happens to be correct ONLY for block-kind lines (`ownShiftUnits === 0` there,
+   invisible on a heading-only smoke test), silently wrong for anything margin-shifted.
+   Fixed by reconstructing `ownShiftUnits` from the line's own fact (`isListItem` →
+   `supplementalDepth`, `isAtom` → `depth`, else 0) the same way `lineDecoration()` already
+   does, inside the live-override path too.
+
+New tests added for both: a heading-with-list-item-child fixture (the exact shape that
+exposed bug 2) checking the marker/guide alignment invariant survives the fold-gap
+interaction, plus the existing chevron-overlap check extended across three heading depths.
+All pass; full suite green (127 unit tests, 71 e2e tests across all 8 spec files).
+
+**Experiment 5b close-out.** Final design: one uniform dot, `var(--text-faint)` (matching
+the guide lines), on every eligible kind, positioned to clear both the node's own
+indentation column and (for headings) the native fold chevron, vertically centered on the
+node's own first rendered visual row, and horizontally centered on any bridging guide line
+from a shallower ancestor. Eight real bugs were found and fixed across three follow-up
+rounds after the initial "keep" verdict — none caught by this experiment's own fixture
+corpus or assertions at the time they shipped; every one came from a human looking at real
+rendered output — a shared screenshot, in most cases — and pushing back on what they saw.
+Total code cost stayed smaller than the initial 8-shape design despite three rounds
+of bug fixes and the addition of a genuinely new live-measurement subsystem (Y-position,
+fold-gap) that guides never needed — see the "Code cost" paragraph above and the
+`combineExtra`/`computeMarkerAndGuideBg` consolidation in `decorations.ts` for why: fixing
+each successive bug generally *simplified* the code by collapsing near-duplicate
+computations into one shared function, not by adding new special cases. The two most
+consequential lessons — recurring bug shapes, not one-off mistakes — are recorded as
+their own entries in "Non-obvious findings" below, since they apply well beyond this one
+experiment.
 
 ### Open question: shrinking only our own added list margin

36	+### Experiment 5b — CSS-shape markers, reusing 2b's background-layer mechanism
37	+
38	+**Verdict: keep**, pending the head-to-head against 5a. Branched off
39	+`experiment/decorations-2b-guides-css-gradient`. All 9 corpus fixtures (the shared 8, plus
40	+a `quote`-kind fixture promoted into `ALL_DECORATION_FIXTURES` per this experiment's own

44	+71/71 e2e tests green across the full suite (all 8 spec files, including a dedicated
45	+"marker vertical position" sub-suite added after real-vault review — see bug 5 below,
46	+two pixel-exact guide/marker-alignment tests, and a fold-chevron-clearance sub-suite —
47	+see the follow-ups below), plus 127/127 unit tests (`npm test`), including new
48	+`decorate: kind`/`headingGuideDepths` tests. Confirmed live
49	+on 4 real vault notes (headings, paragraphs, lists, checkboxes, wikilinks, a code block, a
50	+callout, a table) — no defects found; markers coexist cleanly with real content, including
51	+multi-line paragraphs and wikilinks immediately after a marked line.
52	+
53	+**Design pivoted once, after a live look at the first version.** The plan's own design
54	+called for a distinct shape+color per eligible kind (8 marks: dot/ring/square/diamond/
55	+plus/tick/wedge/cross across Obsidian's 8 accent colors) — built, screenshotted, and
56	+initially judged reasonable from a distance. On closer real-content review the user's
57	+verdict was that the variety read as **cryptic, not helpful**: eight different shapes and
58	+colors ask a reader to memorize a legend before the marks mean anything, which is the
59	+opposite of legible. Replaced with a single, uniform, solid dot — same color as the guide
60	+lines themselves (`var(--text-faint)`) — for every eligible kind. This is a real design
61	+lesson worth carrying forward past this one experiment: for a piece of chrome whose whole
62	+job is "signal that a node starts here," uniformity communicates that job better than
63	+variety that has no established meaning yet. The per-kind shape system is described below
64	+only for the record (what was tried and why it was reverted), not as the current design —
65	+current code has exactly one shape.
66	+
67	+**Mechanism (current)**: a single `radial-gradient` dot (`markerBackground()` in
68	+`decorations.ts`), same color as guides, one call site for every eligible kind (heading/
69	+paragraph/code/table/callout/quote/html/hr — everything except list-item, which needs no
70	+marker check at all now: eligibility collapsed to "not a list item," full stop, since
71	+every other `NodeKind` gets the identical mark). Exposed as a new `--to-marker` custom
72	+property, painted through the *exact* same `::after` 2b's guides already use
73	+(`background: var(--to-marker, none), var(--to-guides, none);`) — zero new
74	+pseudo-element, zero new DOM, zero new `ViewPlugin`. Gap lines stay explicitly
75	+marker-free (they carry no `decorate()` fact/kind at all — verified by a dedicated e2e
76	+test).
77	+
78	+**The real integration wrinkle, exactly as flagged going in**: `lineDecoration()`'s
79	+`to-decor-guides` gate (driving `position: relative` and the whole `::after` rule) was
80	+guide-only (`guideDepths.length > 0`); changed to `guideDepths.length > 0 || hasMarker` so
81	+a depth-0 node with zero ancestors (e.g. a bare top-level heading, which has no guide of
82	+its own today) still shows a marker — confirmed live and by a dedicated e2e test
83	+(`getLinePseudoComputedStyle` reads a non-empty resolved background at depth 0). This
84	+touches the shared `MarginCompensation` `ViewPlugin` too (for widget-rendered atoms:
85	+table/callout/html/hr), which needed the identical gate change plus setting `--to-marker`
86	+alongside its existing `--to-guides` handling.
87	+
88	+**Bugs found and fixed** (all caught only by actually looking at a rendered screenshot,
89	+not by any DOM/computed-style assertion — directly reinforcing this project's recurring
90	+lesson that visual claims need an actual look, not box-model reasoning alone):
91	+
92	+1. **Depth-0 markers were completely invisible, caught by zooming into a screenshot of
93	+   the `mixed` fixture's top-level `"# Top"` heading.** The marker's `background-position`
94	+   is deliberately negative at shallow depths (it sits `MARKER_GAP` left of the node's own
95	+   indent column, which is column 0 at depth 0) — but a background can *never* paint
96	+   outside its own element's box, regardless of what any ancestor's `overflow`/`contain`
97	+   allows. This is a **stricter** rule than the one guides rely on: guides' own doc comment
98	+   established that a pseudo's box can be *widened* via `left`/`right` to reach a shallower
99	+   column, but a first pass here wrongly assumed the same finding covered a marker's own
100	+   small negative position too, without the box actually being widened for it. Fixed by
101	+   computing `markerShortfall(depth)` — `max(0px, MARKER_RESERVE − depth × unit)`, the same
102	+   `max(0px, ...)` clamp idiom Experiment 1's own table-padding fix established — and
103	+   folding it into `--to-own-shift` (which now activates whenever `ownShiftUnits > 0 ||
104	+   marker`, not just for margin-shifted kinds) *and* into every guide layer's own position
105	+   formula active on the same line (`guideLayer` gained an `extra` parameter), since the
106	+   pseudo's local coordinate origin is one shared thing for the whole box — widening it for
107	+   the marker's sake shifts everything else painted in that same box too. At this module's
108	+   default sizes, only depth 0 actually needs the extra reach — confirmed live that every
109	+   depth ≥ 1 marker rendered correctly even before this fix, which is exactly why the bug
110	+   surfaced only on the flat/mixed fixtures' top-level nodes and not on any nested one.
111	+2. **(Per-kind design, since reverted) The table's diamond mark rendered as a lopsided
112	+   pinwheel**, caught by zooming into a screenshot of the `widget-atoms` fixture — the
113	+   standard 4-gradient diamond recipe requires every quadrant's gradient to list
114	+   `transparent 50%` before the color, consistently across all four; an earlier version
115	+   flipped that order on two of the four quadrants, producing a shape with the wrong
116	+   halves filled.
117	+3. **(Per-kind design, since reverted) The callout's plus-sign rendered as a lopsided "⊥"**
118	+   (vertical bar with the crossbar near its bottom, not its middle), caught by zooming into
119	+   the same `widget-atoms` screenshot. Root cause: the vertical-centering formula used `%`,
120	+   and `background-position`'s `%` component resolves relative to *that specific layer's
121	+   own image size* subtracted from the box — which differed between the vertical bar (a
122	+   full-height image) and the horizontal bar (a much thinner one), so the "same" `50%`
123	+   position landed at two different absolute heights for the two layers making up one
124	+   shape. Invisible on single-line plain `.cm-line`s (box height ≈ marker size, so the
125	+   discrepancy was too small to notice) but glaring on a widget atom whose box spans its
126	+   *entire* rendered height (a multi-line callout, not just its title line).
127	+4. **Heading markers sat noticeably above the native fold chevron on H1–H5, and merely
128	+   collided with it on H6 — reported directly by the user after a real-vault look, not
129	+   caught by any fixture or assertion.** The fix for bug 3 above replaced `%`-based
130	+   vertical centering with a FIXED length from the box's own top, chosen to solve the
131	+   widget-atom case — but a fixed length doesn't scale with a *heading's own* line-height,
132	+   which varies by level (H1's line box is taller than H6's). Measured live, not guessed:
133	+   `.cm-fold-indicator`'s own `getBoundingClientRect()` against its line's, for H1 through
134	+   H6 in a real running Obsidian instance. Two findings: the chevron's own vertical
135	+   *center* sits at a level-dependent ~67–70% down the line box (not a clean, reusable
136	+   constant), but its **top** is a robust, level-independent constant — exactly 1rem below
137	+   the line's own top at every level measured — because the chevron's position is fixed
138	+   while only *its own icon size* (not its position) scales with the heading's font-size.
139	+   First fix: gave heading lines specifically a measured fixed-top anchor (`1rem`, matching
140	+   the chevron's own measured top). Confirmed live via screenshot — no longer "above" on
141	+   any level — but this fix didn't survive the NEXT round of real-vault review (bug 5).
142	+5. **The whole "one CSS formula per box-type" approach was still wrong — a full-suite
143	+   real-vault review found FIVE distinct placement failures at once, all reported directly
144	+   by the user, none caught by any fixture/assertion.** Headings still sat above their own
145	+   text (the bug-4 fixed-top anchor didn't move with a font-size-driven line-height
146	+   *increase* the same way the chevron's OWN top didn't, but the user's own read of "middle
147	+   of the heading text" wanted the TEXT's center, and a bare fixed length can't track a
148	+   font-size it has no knowledge of at layout time); single-line paragraphs sat at their own
149	+   top edge (the still-present double-`HALF`-subtraction bug from bug 3, never actually
150	+   fixed for the plain-line case, only worked around for widget atoms); wrapped multi-row
151	+   paragraphs/blockquotes centered on their ENTIRE wrapped height instead of just the first
152	+   visual row; callouts and code blocks sat at the literal top of the whole block instead of
153	+   near their own first content row. Root cause, named directly by the user: **no CSS-only
154	+   formula (percentage, fixed length, or any mix) can know where "the first rendered visual
155	+   row of this specific box's content" actually is** — that's fundamentally a *rendering*
156	+   fact, not a static layout constant, and every earlier fix in this area was really just a
157	+   differently-shaped guess at it. Fixed by abandoning CSS-only positioning for `Y` entirely
158	+   and measuring it live: `MarginCompensation` (already the established "StateField draws a
159	+   default, ViewPlugin patches in the true live-read value" mechanism for `nativeMarginBasePx`
160	+   and table-padding compensation) now computes a real per-line pixel offset via
161	+   `document.createRange().selectNodeContents(lineEl); range.getClientRects()[0]` — the DOM's
162	+   own notion of an element's first wrapped visual row, which handles word-wrap for free and
163	+   scales with whatever font-size is actually in effect, no guessing required. Written to a
164	+   new `--to-decor-marker-y` custom property; `markerBackground()` itself only ever emits a
165	+   `var(--to-decor-marker-y, 50%)` reference (the `50%` fallback is now CORRECTLY centering,
166	+   not double-subtracted, for the brief window before the ViewPlugin's own pass overrides
167	+   it). Widget atoms get the same technique aimed at a kind-appropriate reference element
168	+   instead of the widget's own root (a callout's `.callout-title`, a table's first `<tr>`;
169	+   `hr` has no text content at all, so its own half-height stands in; `html`, arbitrary raw
170	+   markup with no fixed structure, falls back to the same first-row Range technique applied
171	+   to its own root). One deliberate, documented exception: a code fence's structural first
172	+   `.cm-line` is the OPENING marker row (confirmed live to be a REAL, normal-height row —
173	+   not collapsed to zero — that Live Preview just renders with nothing but a language badge
174	+   on it, with the actual code text starting on the NEXT line down) — reaching into that next
175	+   line for a "true" measurement was rejected because this pseudo-element's own box is
176	+   exactly ONE line tall and a background can never paint outside its own box (the same hard
177	+   rule bug 1 already established), so doing this safely would need the same kind of
178	+   vertical box-widening `--to-own-shift` already does horizontally, introducing an unverified
179	+   NEW paint-order risk against the next (opaque-backgrounded) code line. Per an explicit user
180	+   ask ("if that is harder to calculate, let's try an offset, to make it a bit lower"), code
181	+   fences instead bias toward the bottom of their own (still fully in-bounds, zero clipping
182	+   risk) opener row via a plain `95%` — confirmed live via computed style (`background-position:
183	+   12px 95%`) to land within ~1px of the next line's own top, a pragmatic near-miss rather than
184	+   an exact, mechanism-stretching alignment. All five cases now covered by dedicated e2e tests
185	+   (see `52-block-markers-shapes.e2e.ts`'s "marker vertical position" suite) reading the
186	+   resolved `background-position` directly, not just the raw custom property.
187	+
188	+**Code cost (current, single-dot design + live-measured Y)**: ~110 lines added to
189	+`decorations.ts` (marker position/color constants, `markerBackground()`, `firstRowCenterPx`/
190	+`widgetMarkerYPx`, and the `lineDecoration()`/`MarginCompensation` integration — still
191	+meaningfully SMALLER than the original 8-shape version's ~150 lines despite the added
192	+live-measurement code, since one shape needs no per-kind switch, no quadrant/tick/bar helper
193	+functions, and no color table), ~1 line changed in `styles.css` (the `::after` rule's
194	+`background` shorthand, plus an expanded doc comment), ~10 lines added to `decorate.ts`
195	+(`kind` field on `LineDecorationFact` — still used by `MarginCompensation`'s widget-Y
196	+dispatch and the code-fence Y exception, even though `markerBackground()` itself no longer
197	+branches on kind). Reuses 100% of 2b's existing plumbing (custom property → single
198	+`::after`, `--to-own-shift` compensation, `MarginCompensation`'s widget-DOM patching loop)
199	+for X-axis positioning and shape/color — no new CM6 mechanism, no new pseudo-element, no
200	+new `ViewPlugin`. Y-axis positioning is a GENUINELY new piece of live-measurement code
201	+(not reused from guides, which never needed it), the first real net-new mechanism this
202	+experiment added rather than reused. The one real *design* cost independent of Y (not
203	+code-size cost) is that `--to-own-shift` and every guide layer's X position formula are no
204	+longer independent of whether a marker is *also* active on the same line — a coupling 5a's
205	+separate-DOM-element mechanism wouldn't have, since a real DOM child element doesn't need
206	+to share a single pseudo-element's one coordinate space with the guide gradient.
207	+
208	+**Known limitation, not fixed on this branch**: markers and guides share one `::after`'s
209	+single `opacity: 0.6`, inherited from the guide rule (subtle-on-purpose, for a background
210	+grid). A marker dot at 60% opacity is legible but visibly softer than surrounding text —
211	+CSS has no way to give two background layers on the *same* element independent opacity.
212	+Splitting them (e.g. painting the marker at full opacity some other way) would cost exactly
213	+the "claim a third pseudo-element" move this experiment's own design explicitly ruled out
214	+as unavailable. Worth a deliberate follow-up decision (accept the shared opacity, or
215	+promote markers off `::after` onto a real DOM node as 5a already does) once both variants
216	+are compared side by side.
217	+
218	+**Follow-up refinement: guide columns now align with a marker's own CENTER, not the raw
219	+depth boundary**, requested by the user to read more like native nested lists (a connecting
220	+line running straight through each bullet, not stopping short of it). Previously a guide at
221	+ancestor depth `d` was drawn at column `d * unit` exactly — the same column the NEXT depth
222	+level's indentation starts at, which is also where a marker's own reach calculation
223	+happened to START (its left edge), not where it visually CENTERS. This left a real, visible
224	+gap: the guide line sat to the right of the marker dot above/below it, a seam rather than a
225	+continuous line. Fixed by shifting every guide's own column left by `MARKER_HALF +
226	+MARKER_GAP` (`GUIDE_COLUMN_OFFSET`) — exactly the offset between a marker's own depth
227	+column and its rendered center (confirmed live, pixel-exact: a dedicated e2e test reads
228	+both a heading's own marker center and its descendant's guide column as independent
229	+absolute screen positions — accounting for the pseudo-element's own `left` CSS offset
230	+*and* `background-position`, a real bug in the test's first draft, not the implementation —
231	+and asserts they match within 1px).
232	+
233	+This shift itself went negative for shallow (in practice, depth-0) ancestors, the same
234	+"background can't paint outside its own box" constraint markers already ran into — fixed by
235	+generalizing the existing `markerShortfall`/`--to-own-shift` widening mechanism
236	+(`combineExtra`) to also cover the shallowest active guide's own reach, not just a line's
237	+own marker. The two are independent reasons a line's pseudo box might need widening (its
238	+OWN marker's left edge; a shallow ancestor's now marker-center-aligned guide column) that
239	+happen to share one box, so `combineExtra` takes the `max()` of whichever apply. This also
240	+meant a blank trailingGap line (which never carries a marker, only ever a guide) can now
241	+independently need `--to-own-shift` too — previously true only for a small minority of
242	+gap lines with genuinely shallow guides, but the mechanism generalizes without needing a
243	+gap-specific carve-out.
244	+
245	+**Real bug shipped in the above fix, caught by the user from a real-vault screenshot
246	+(not by the dedicated alignment test) — mid-chain markers drifted visibly left of their
247	+own guide column.** The screenshot showed a real note (`Wednesday — review day` → `Aurora
248	+review` → paragraphs): the FIRST guide (from the top-level heading) correctly passed
249	+through the second level's marker, but every level from there down drifted — "the bullets
250	+(from the second level on) are shifted to the left, while the guides are evenly spaced,"
251	+in the user's own words, correctly diagnosing that the guides themselves were fine and the
252	+markers were the ones off. Root cause: the fix above computes ONE combined `extra` per
253	+line — `max` of the line's own marker shortfall and its shallowest active guide's shortfall
254	+— and that combined value is what the box is ACTUALLY widened by (`--to-own-shift`). The
255	+marker's own X-position formula must use that SAME combined value for the cancellation
256	+algebra to hold (`--to-own-shift` and the position formula need to widen/shift by identical
257	+amounts, or the difference leaks into the rendered position) — but the code built the
258	+marker's background string from its own (narrower) `markerShortfall` alone, computed
259	+*before* combining with the guide's requirement, then combined separately only for
260	+`--to-own-shift`. For a depth-0 node this is invisible (no guide of its own exists to
261	+diverge from, so the two values coincide) — exactly the case the dedicated alignment test
262	+used, which is why it passed despite the bug. For a MID-chain node like "Aurora review"
263	+(depth 1, carries both its own marker AND an active depth-0 guide bridging through it),
264	+its own shortfall alone is 0 (depth 1 doesn't need widening for its OWN marker), but the
265	+combined value is nonzero (the depth-0 guide does need it) — using the narrower value for
266	+the marker's position while the box widens by the larger one drifted the marker left by
267	+exactly `MARKER_RESERVE` (~12px at default sizes), confirmed by hand-deriving the algebra
268	+and matching it against live computed-style output before fixing. Fixed by computing the
269	+combined `extra` FIRST and using it for both the box widening AND the marker's own X
270	+formula. New e2e test added specifically for this shape (heading → heading → paragraph,
271	+three levels) rather than trusting the depth-0/depth-1 case again — verifies every
272	+ancestor's marker center against its immediate child's guide column, not just the
273	+top-level pair. A concrete, second instance of this project's own recurring lesson: a
274	+real multi-level scenario is a different (and necessary) test from its simplest two-level
275	+stand-in, and a screenshot a human actually looked at caught what a passing assertion
276	+suite didn't.
277	+
278	+**Follow-up: the native fold chevron overlapped our marker on headings, fixed with a
279	+second, real multi-part bug of its own.** Reported directly by the user: Obsidian's own
280	+fold/collapse indicator (`.collapse-indicator.collapse-icon` — the actual glyph; its
281	+wrapper, `.cm-fold-indicator`, measured 0 width live and isn't itself a useful reference)
282	+sits in the exact same pixels our marker does, on every heading (confirmed live: the
283	+chevron's own gutter is reserved even on a heading with no children yet, painted at
284	+`opacity: 0` until hovered/foldable — so the reach is needed unconditionally, not only once
285	+a heading actually has a child, which also avoids the marker jumping sideways the moment
286	+one is added). Its own width doesn't vary by heading level (confirmed live, H1 through H3
287	+identical in the bundled theme), unlike its vertical position, so it's measured ONCE per
288	+render from any one heading line and applied uniformly — the same "measure a single
289	+representative reference" pattern `nativeMarginBasePx` already established, not a new one.
290	+
291	+Fixed by folding this reach into the SAME `markerShortfall`/`--to-own-shift` mechanism
292	+markers already use (not a parallel, independently-applied offset — an earlier draft of
293	+this exact fix tried that shape and it silently double-subtracted). Two real bugs shipped
294	+in getting there, both caught by the user pushing on the result rather than by any
295	+assertion in this experiment's own suite at the time:
296	+
297	+1. **Shifting a heading's own marker left of the chevron broke every DESCENDANT's guide
298	+   column, which still pointed at the marker's OLD (un-shifted) center.** A first version
299	+   only recomputed the fold-adjusted marker on the heading's OWN line, live, via
300	+   `MarginCompensation` — correct for that one line in isolation, but guides (already fixed
301	+   to align with a marker's CENTER, see above) are computed independently on every
302	+   DESCENDANT line, with no way to know the ancestor's marker had moved. Root cause,
303	+   structurally: only HEADINGS (and list items, which never own a guide at all) can fold in
304	+   Obsidian's own UI — a `paragraph`-owned guide (this project's tree lets a paragraph have
305	+   children too) never needs the extra reach, so the fix couldn't be a single per-line
306	+   constant; it needed to know, PER ACTIVE GUIDE DEPTH on a given line, whether THAT
307	+   specific depth's owning ancestor is a heading. Fixed by extending `computeLineGuides`
308	+   (decorate.ts) with a new `headingGuideDepths` field — the subset of `guideDepths` whose
309	+   owner is a heading — and generalizing `guideShortfall`/`guideLayer`/`guideBackground`/
310	+   `combineExtra` to accept a per-depth fold-gap reach instead of one shared value. Checking
311	+   only the shallowest active guide depth (the pre-existing optimization, valid without
312	+   fold-gap since shortfall strictly decreases with depth) also had to go: fold-gap breaks
313	+   that monotonicity, since it applies only to SOME depths — a deeper heading-owned guide
314	+   can now need more reach than a shallower paragraph-owned one on the same line, so
315	+   `combineExtra` checks every active depth, not just the first. The live-measurement pass
316	+   in `MarginCompensation` was correspondingly widened from "the heading's own line only" to
317	+   "every line that carries a marker OR bridges a heading-owned guide" — consolidated
318	+   through one new shared function, `computeMarkerAndGuideBg`, so `lineDecoration()`,
319	+   `gapLineDecoration()`, the widget loop, and this live pass all funnel through the exact
320	+   same computation (foldGapPx defaulting to 0 for the three static callers) rather than
321	+   four independently-maintained copies.
322	+2. **The fix for bug 1 then dropped the `ownShiftUnits * unit` term for margin-shifted
323	+   lines (atoms/list items) when recomputing `--to-own-shift` live**, caught by a dedicated
324	+   e2e test (not a screenshot this time — the discrepancy was small enough, and the
325	+   fixture specific enough, that a computed-style assertion was the right tool) failing by
326	+   exactly one `--to-decor-unit` (24px at default sizes): `--to-own-shift` for these kinds
327	+   must equal `ownShiftUnits * unit + extra` (their OWN depth-based margin shift, plus the
328	+   marker/guide widening) — the new general live-override path set it to `extra` alone,
329	+   which happens to be correct ONLY for block-kind lines (`ownShiftUnits === 0` there,
330	+   invisible on a heading-only smoke test), silently wrong for anything margin-shifted.
331	+   Fixed by reconstructing `ownShiftUnits` from the line's own fact (`isListItem` →
332	+   `supplementalDepth`, `isAtom` → `depth`, else 0) the same way `lineDecoration()` already
333	+   does, inside the live-override path too.
334	+
335	+New tests added for both: a heading-with-list-item-child fixture (the exact shape that
336	+exposed bug 2) checking the marker/guide alignment invariant survives the fold-gap
337	+interaction, plus the existing chevron-overlap check extended across three heading depths.
338	+All pass; full suite green (127 unit tests, 71 e2e tests across all 8 spec files).
339	+
340	+**Experiment 5b close-out.** Final design: one uniform dot, `var(--text-faint)` (matching
341	+the guide lines), on every eligible kind, positioned to clear both the node's own
342	+indentation column and (for headings) the native fold chevron, vertically centered on the
343	+node's own first rendered visual row, and horizontally centered on any bridging guide line
344	+from a shallower ancestor. Eight real bugs were found and fixed across three follow-up
345	+rounds after the initial "keep" verdict — none caught by this experiment's own fixture
346	+corpus or assertions at the time they shipped; every one came from a human looking at real
347	+rendered output — a shared screenshot, in most cases — and pushing back on what they saw.
348	+Total code cost stayed smaller than the initial 8-shape design despite three rounds
349	+of bug fixes and the addition of a genuinely new live-measurement subsystem (Y-position,
350	+fold-gap) that guides never needed — see the "Code cost" paragraph above and the
351	+`combineExtra`/`computeMarkerAndGuideBg` consolidation in `decorations.ts` for why: fixing
352	+each successive bug generally *simplified* the code by collapsing near-duplicate
353	+computations into one shared function, not by adding new special cases. The two most
354	+consequential lessons — recurring bug shapes, not one-off mistakes — are recorded as
355	+their own entries in "Non-obvious findings" below, since they apply well beyond this one
356	+experiment.
357	 
358	 ### Open question: shrinking only our own added list margin
359	 
360	@@ -735,7 +1059,7 @@ kind of within-list misalignment the wide-numbering fixture exists to catch.
361	 ## Non-obvious findings
362	 
363	 Cross-experiment learnings, carried forward the same way the postmortem's own "carried-forward
364	-technical findings" section was meant to be used — read this before starting 2a/2b/3/4:
365	+technical findings" section was meant to be used — read this before starting 2a/2b/3/4/5a/5b:
366	 
367	 - **CM6 `Decoration.line` has zero effect on Obsidian's "embed-block" replacement widgets**
368	   (tables, callouts, raw HTML, and horizontal rules) — not a partial win, not a class-merge;
369	@@ -883,6 +1207,71 @@ technical findings" section was meant to be used — read this before starting 2
370	   final rule — silently losing a piece that was necessary but easy to assume the new rule
371	   subsumed. Caught only because a human tried the rebuilt output and reported a regression,
372	   not because the "surely this is equivalent" reasoning was double-checked before shipping it.
373	+- **When a box's own position is widened to reach a coordinate outside itself (2b's own
374	+  `--to-own-shift` technique above), EVERY layer painted on that box — and the widening
375	+  amount itself — must be driven from ONE shared value, computed ONCE.** This is the single
376	+  most-repeated bug shape across Experiment 5b, showing up in three structurally different
377	+  guises: (1) a marker's own position went unwidened while its box wasn't, so it clipped
378	+  entirely at shallow depths; (2) after guides were changed to align with a marker's
379	+  *center* rather than the raw depth column, the marker's own position formula kept using
380	+  its OWN (narrower) shortfall while the box widened by the marker-vs-guide COMBINED one,
381	+  drifting the marker off its intended column at any depth where the two values diverge;
382	+  (3) after folding in the native fold-chevron's reach, a live-override code path set
383	+  `--to-own-shift` to the combined `extra` alone, silently dropping the OTHER term
384	+  (`ownShiftUnits * unit`) that margin-shifted lines also need there. All three are the
385	+  *same* mistake at heart — two call sites that are supposed to derive from one number
386	+  quietly grew independent, slightly different formulas — and all three were fixed the
387	+  same way: consolidate into one function (`computeMarkerAndGuideBg`) that every caller,
388	+  static and live, goes through, so there is structurally only one place left to get it
389	+  wrong. Worth treating as a standing design rule for any future decoration needing
390	+  box-widening, not a lesson specific to markers or guides: **when two things must move
391	+  together, make it impossible to change one without the other, don't just remember to.**
392	+- **`background-position`'s `%` component already resolves relative to (box size − image
393	+  size) — it does NOT need a manual `- HALF`-style correction to "center" something.**
394	+  `background-position: 50% 50%` alone centers an image; subtracting half the image's own
395	+  size on top of that shifts it further than intended. This is an easy mistake to make by
396	+  analogy with *length*-based positioning (where a literal offset does need that
397	+  correction, since a length value places the image's top-left corner directly, with no
398	+  automatic size-aware adjustment) — the two positioning modes have different semantics and
399	+  don't compose the way intuition suggests. Caught only because the user reported a marker
400	+  sitting suspiciously close to a line's top edge on real content; a synthetic single-line
401	+  fixture at the exact right size could easily have looked "close enough" to pass a casual
402	+  screenshot glance.
403	+- **Measure the actual visual element a fix needs to clear, not its wrapper — a wrapper can
404	+  report a technically-true but practically-useless size (here, 0).** Obsidian's native fold
405	+  chevron is two nested elements: `.cm-fold-indicator` (a zero-width anchor point Obsidian
406	+  positions at the text's own start column) and `.collapse-indicator.collapse-icon` (the
407	+  actual glyph, rendered via a negative offset extending LEFT of that anchor). Measuring the
408	+  wrapper's own `getBoundingClientRect()` would have silently produced a no-op fix (0px of
409	+  extra reach); the real, non-zero width was only on the child. Generalizes: when a fix
410	+  needs "how much space does native UI element X occupy," verify empirically which specific
411	+  DOM node actually carries that dimension — a "reasonable-sounding" selector (the outer
412	+  wrapper, the one with the semantic class name) is not guaranteed to be the right one.
413	+- **A per-line "extra reach" constant is the wrong shape once a chrome adjustment applies to
414	+  only SOME of the ancestors bridging that line, not all of them uniformly.** The fold-gap
415	+  fix initially treated "does this line need extra reach for a fold chevron" as one
416	+  per-line yes/no fact — true for a single node's own marker, but WRONG for guides, where
417	+  one line can simultaneously bridge a heading-owned ancestor (needs the reach) and a
418	+  paragraph-owned one (doesn't — paragraphs never fold in Obsidian's UI, even though this
419	+  project's own tree lets them own children). The fix needed to track the adjustment
420	+  PER ACTIVE ANCESTOR DEPTH, not per line — `computeLineGuides` grew a `headingGuideDepths`
421	+  field (the subset of `guideDepths` whose owner is foldable) precisely so a downstream
422	+  per-depth decision could be made correctly. A useful general question when a new
423	+  chrome-clearing adjustment is added to an EXISTING per-line-aggregate mechanism (any
424	+  future guide/marker refinement included): "does this adjustment apply uniformly to every
425	+  contributor to this aggregate, or only some of them?" — if only some, the aggregate needs
426	+  to carry per-contributor detail, not just a combined total.
427	+- **A drastic design simplification (8 distinct shapes+colors → 1 uniform dot) is a
428	+  legitimate outcome of a "build it, then actually look at it" process, not a failure of
429	+  the original design.** The plan called for a distinct mark per kind specifically to test
430	+  whether that was legible; building it, screenshotting it, and then judging — correctly —
431
